equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: delivery-ready entries are collected directly from the pool without a pre-collection deadline guard or RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK cap.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing Phase C skip when the build deadline has already elapsed and missing configurable cap/default 64 on delivery-ready transaction collection.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/payload/src/lib.rs defines global_deadline and later checks it before sequencing/execution, but still calls pool.get_delivery_ready_original_async_transactions(current_block) unconditionally.
crates/transaction-pool/src/pool/mod.rs returns all delivery-ready originals for the current block with no max argument or env cap.
The only take(max) hits are ordinary transaction-pool APIs unrelated to delivery-ready async settlement collection.

rationale: The diff mirrors the fulfilled-async guard for Phase C because each delivery entry performs contract reads, expiry checks, and settlement creation. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If the payload build deadline has elapsed before Phase C, delivery settlement collection is skipped; otherwise delivery-ready transactions are capped by `RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK`, defaulting to 64.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set `RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK=1` with multiple delivery-ready jobs and assert only one delivery settlement is collected; force the global deadline expired and assert Phase C returns no delivery-ready transactions.
summary: Delivery settlement collection is currently unbounded by the requested deadline/cap behavior.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK=1 with multiple DeliveryReady jobs; current local collection has no env-var cap to enforce one entry.
title: Delivery settlement collection is bounded
